title: ProteinGym Deep Mutational Scanning and Clinical Variant Benchmarks

data_urls.description: ProteinGym Open Data on AWS registry
data_urls.access_notes: Provides parquet datasets and benchmark assets for ProteinGym.
Use repository metadata and scripts for benchmark-compatible loading.

instructions: Start with one DMS assay or one protein family rather than loading every ProteinGym asset.
Record assay ID, wild-type sequence, mutation notation, fitness column, and benchmark version.
When using clinical variants, record source database and phenotype semantics separately from DMS fitness.

scale.download_size: millions of variants across many assays; task-specific subsets are recommended

recommended_axes.name: sequence_identity
recommended_axes.definition: Protein sequence identity or edit distance to training variants.

recommended_axes.name: mutation_position_support
recommended_axes.definition: Distance from training-mutated positions, mutation count, or local sequence context support.

recommended_axes.name: structural_site_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Distance to active sites, domains, or structural neighborhoods when structures or annotations are available.

recommended_axes.name: assay_family_similarity
recommended_axes.definition: Similarity across DMS assays, protein families, measurement protocols, or phenotype types.

useful_for: Auditing protein language models and mutation-effect predictors.
Testing whether random variant splits overstate extrapolation to unseen positions or proteins.
Constructing sequence, structure, and assay-context SPECTRA curves.
leakage_risks: Variants from the same assay or protein can leak family-specific context.
Training on single mutants can leak information into multiple-mutant evaluation if mutation support is ignored.
Clinical and DMS labels have different semantics and should not be mixed without a declared task.
